title: PutObject, Metadata, Checksums, and Encryption Defaults · Language Lens: Content Metadata Preservation
summary: A service uploads an unbounded reader, uses the returned ETag as a universal digest, loses content metadata on replacement, and cannot prove which bytes S3 accepted. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the content metadata preservation increment in single-object upload and integrity evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Set and preserve Content-Type, Content-Disposition, Cache-Control, encoding, language, custom metadata, and tags during replacements and copies.
Select supported checksum algorithms, send full-object evidence, validate service response, and retain an application integrity record.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Content Metadata Preservation · Theory Content Metadata Preservation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 2092 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove accepted bytes, checksum, metadata, and encryption without using ETag as a universal digest; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Set and preserve Content-Type, Content-Disposition, Cache-Control, encoding, language, custom metadata, and tags during replacements and copies.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Content Metadata Preservation contract for the supplied data.
